reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All three FP gates passed. The finding identifies a genuine code defect where locally-produced data columns bypass the anti-equivocation cache (broadcastAndReceiveDataColumns:L502-504 calls ReceiveDataColumns without calling setSeenDataColumnIndex), while the P2P gossip path correctly updates the cache (receiveDataColumnSidecar:L42). An attacker controlling a validator can trigger this by producing and broadcasting data columns, causing cache inconsistency that could allow duplicate column acceptance or equivocation detection bypass. Severity downgraded from High to Medium because Prysm's 31% network share is below the 33% threshold required for High severity per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json severity classification.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Anti-equivocation cache must be updated for all imported data columns regardless of publishing (invariant SG-010)
